Gap-filling of climate data with station-wise machine learning

Source: R/sus_climate_fill_gap.R
sus_climate_fill_gaps.Rd

This function performs data-driven gap-filling (statistical imputation) in climate and air-quality time series using gradient boosting (XGBoost).

The method:

  • Trains one separate model per station and per target variable;

  • Uses automatic temporal feature engineering (lags and rolling windows);

  • Applies station-level quality filtering before modeling;

  • Supports multiple data sources (e.g., INMET, NOAA, AirQuality);

  • Can run in parallel across stations;

  • Returns both filled data and processing metadata.

Important methodological notes:

  • This is a gap-filling (imputation) method, NOT a forecasting model. Predictions are only made for timestamps where data are missing.

  • Models are trained only on observed (non-missing) data within each station.

  • No information from the future is used to impute past values (only lagged and rolling features are used).

  • If a station exceeds the allowed missingness threshold for a variable, that variable is not imputed for that station.

Usage

sus_climate_fill_gaps(
  df,
  target_var,
  datetime_col = NULL,
  station_col = NULL,
  source = "auto",
  quality_threshold = 0.4,
  lag_periods = c(3, 6, 12, 24),
  rolling_windows = c(3, 6, 12),
  run_evaluation = FALSE,
  gap_percentage = 0.2,
  gap_mechanism = "MCAR",
  keep_features = FALSE,
  model_params = list(),
  n_workers = NULL,
  verbose = TRUE,
  lang = "pt"
)

Arguments

df

A data.frame (or tibble) containing climate or air-quality data. Must include:

  • a datetime column (POSIXct, POSIXlt, or convertible to datetime),

  • a station identifier column,

  • at least one numeric target variable to be filled.

target_var

Character vector. One or more variable names to be gap-filled. Each variable is modeled and imputed independently. Example: c("tair_dry_bulb_c", "rh_mean_porc", "ws_2_m_s").

datetime_col

Character. Name of the datetime column in df. If NULL (default), the function attempts automatic detection.

station_col

Character. Name of the station identifier column. If NULL, it is auto-detected based on the declared source.

source

Character. Data source identifier:

  • "INMET" (Brazilian weather stations),

  • "NOAA" (global weather stations),

  • "AirQuality" (air pollution data),

  • "auto" (default): automatically detected from column patterns.

quality_threshold

Numeric. Maximum allowed proportion of missing values per station and per variable. Stations exceeding this threshold are excluded for that variable. Default: 0.4 (40% missing).

lag_periods

Numeric vector. Time lags (in hours) used to construct predictors. Default: c(3, 6, 12, 24).

rolling_windows

Numeric vector. Window sizes (in hours) for rolling statistics (mean and standard deviation). Default: c(3, 6, 12).

run_evaluation

Logical. If TRUE, performs an internal missing-data simulation to assess imputation performance (RMSE / MAE). Default: FALSE.

gap_percentage

Numeric. If run_evaluation = TRUE, proportion of observed data artificially removed to test performance. Default: 0.2 (20%).

gap_mechanism

Character. Missing-data mechanism used in evaluation when run_evaluation = TRUE:

  • "MCAR": Missing Completely At Random (default),

  • "MAR": Missing At Random,

  • "MNAR": Missing Not At Random.

keep_features

Logical. If TRUE, retains engineered features (lags, rolling statistics, and temporal variables). If FALSE (default), only original columns + is_imputed_* are returned.

model_params

List of additional XGBoost parameters to override defaults. Example: list(max_depth = 4, eta = 0.05, nrounds = 200)

n_workers

Integer. Number of parallel workers. If NULL (default), uses availableCores() - 1.

verbose

Logical. If TRUE, prints progress messages and warnings.

lang

Character. Language for messages: "pt", "en", or "es". Default: "pt".

Value

An object of class "climasus_df" with updated metadata, containing:

  • data: original data with filled values,

  • is_imputed_: logical flag per variable indicating which values were imputed,

  • climasus_meta: metadata including:

    • data source,

    • number of imputed values,

    • imputation rate,

    • processing time,

    • quality threshold used.

Details

Fill gaps in climate and air-quality time series using XGBoost

Examples

if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{
# Basic usage (single variable)
filled_data <- sus_climate_fill_gaps(
  df = inmet_climate,
  target_var = "tair_dry_bulb_c"
)

# Multiple variables in one call
filled_data <- sus_climate_fill_gaps(
  df = climate_data,
  target_var = c("tair_dry_bulb_c", "rh_mean_porc", "ws_2_m_s"),
  quality_threshold = 0.4
)

# With performance evaluation
filled_data <- sus_climate_fill_gaps(
  df = climate_data,
  target_var = "tair_dry_bulb_c",
  run_evaluation = TRUE,
  gap_percentage = 0.2
)
} # }
